nodalMinistryName.label: Ministry Of Social Justice and Empowerment

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: Under the National Social Assistance Program (NFBS) by the Government of India, Ministry of Rural Development, Department of Rural Development, such members of the families living below the poverty line whose natural/unnatural death, for the purpose of providing social security to the victim's family Financial aid is provided.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - Eligible applicants are entitled to a lump sum grant of ₹ 30,000/
schemeContent.briefDescription: This scheme is being executed for the family members who are unable to make their livelihood after the death of the primary breadwinner of the family.

children.text: Under the National Social Assistance Program (NFBS) by the Government of India, Ministry of Rural Development, Department of Rural Development, such members of the families living below the poverty line whose natural/unnatural death, for the purpose of providing social security to the victim's family Financial aid is provided.
Eligible applicants are entitled to a lump sum grant of ₹ 30,000/
Applicants have to visit Gram Panchayat/Janpad Panchyat for rural Areas and Nagar Nigam/Nagar Panchayat/Nagar Parishad for urban areas.
Applicants have to get the application form and fill out the application form with the required information.
Applicants have to submit the application form along with all the necessary documents to the office of
Gram Panchayat/Janpad Panchyat for rural areas.
Nagar Nigam/Nagar Panchayat/Nagar Parishad for urban areas
On receipt of the application, the application will be sent by the designated officer to the Gram Panchayat in the rural area and to the urban body in the urban area for investigation.

The application received for the above investigation will be sent to the concerned Designated Officer by the Gram Panchayat in case of rural area and by the concerned urban body in the case of urban area after getting the investigation done by the concerned officer /employee as per the rules.

On being eligible on the basis of the report, the assistance amount will be accepted by the designated officer and the assistance amount will be paid to the concerned person in his bank account, this payment will be done within the time limit of the application date, and the information will be given to the applicant.

On being ineligible on the basis of the report, the application will be rejected by the designated officer and clear information along with the reason will be given to the applicant in the prescribed format.

applicationProcess.process_md:
1. Applicants have to visit Gram Panchayat/Janpad Panchyat for rural Areas and Nagar Nigam/Nagar Panchayat/Nagar Parishad for urban areas.
1. Applicants have to get the application form and fill out the application form with the required information. 
1. Applicants have to submit the application form along with all the necessary documents to the office of 

   1. Gram Panchayat/Janpad Panchyat for rural areas.
   1. Nagar Nigam/Nagar Panchayat/Nagar Parishad for urban areas 

1. On receipt of the application, the application will be sent by the designated officer to the Gram Panchayat in the rural area and to the urban body in the urban area for investigation. 
1. The application received for the above investigation will be sent to the concerned Designated Officer by the Gram Panchayat in case of rural area and by the concerned urban body in the case of urban area after getting the investigation done by the concerned officer /employee as per the rules.
1. On being eligible on the basis of the report, the assistance amount will be accepted by the designated officer and the assistance amount will be paid to the concerned person in his bank account, this payment will be done within the time limit of the application date, and the information will be given to the applicant.
1. On being ineligible on the basis of the report, the application will be rejected by the designated officer and clear information along with the reason will be given to the applicant in the prescribed format.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The scheme is applicable in case of the death of the primary earning head of the family (male or female). 1. The deceased earning member should be above 18 years and below 60 years of age at the time of death. 1. The annual family income should not exceed ₹46,080 in rural areas. 1. The annual family income should not exceed ₹56,460 in urban areas. 1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttar Pradesh.

children.text: The scheme is applicable in case of the death of the primary earning head of the family (male or female).
The deceased earning member should be above 18 years and below 60 years of age at the time of death.
The annual family income should not exceed ₹46,080 in rural areas.
The annual family income should not exceed ₹56,460 in urban areas.
The applicant must be a permanent resident of Uttar Pradesh.
